item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is boring object whithout interesting anomaly and/or history of this object. I don't know why it has got lots of upvotes. Downvote.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Unfortunately, the image originates from here, which holds all content under all rights reserved. As such, it is not compliant with CC 3.0 and has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like Dr. Bright is hiding a pair.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He becomes a superhero whenever someone snaps there fingers. Also he becomes a superhero he doesn't just thinks he is one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know that comic series Captain Underpants? This is the same thing. An average Joe puts on underwear and becomes convinced that they're a superhero. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoy things like this. I was sad when the last underwear scp was deleted. Glad to see this one is still up there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That added table for previous and next SCPs doesn't look very good to me. I also don't really see the need for it in the first place; I didn't try to read all the articles as fast as possible when I first joined.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't care all that much for the idea and the writing really irks me in some places. Sorry, but it's a downvote for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah, my apologies. ( Can barely read English )
Anyways, ideas good the wordings good I'd upvote if it didn't make me think so much of the katana SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The third [effect] is a compulsive effect whereby any person who views the garment will wish to wear it. All subjects, when questioned, have given responses related to nostalgia and/or the thrill of being a child again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I might have missed this, but what effect does the SCP have when viewed? Otherwise the 'Blurring' effect over the clothing is rather unnecessary, if compulsive I'd put that in somewhere in the description.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That just made me giggle soda out of my nose

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very well said. Now please come relay this to some art students I know who cried at the last critique :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, I appreciate that. :) I try and be mindful of a few things when receiving feedback/critique:

  • I am confident in my abilities as a writer.
  • I don't have any reason to take criticism personally as nobody here knows me.
  • Everyone I've encountered thus far offering critique has been genuinely trying to make me a better writer.
  • I can't please everyone at the same time, but that doesn't mean I should ignore conflicting advice. It's better to see both sides and decide what fits the tone/head-canon of what I'm trying to put out there.

The best advice I can give with doing your first entry is show it to the chat room first. Put it in the sandbox and take your initial lumps. The tone is very, very specific; you don't realize the level of precision involved until you sit down to write one of your own. The chatroom is home to a lot of highly-rated authors that actively set the Foundation universe in its place. The first and second draft of this had it as a Factory item, but someone that had written 5 Factory articles pointed out that all the Factory-produced stuff had a theoretical upside to its use.

Also, writing one for the sake of having your SCP article on the Internet doesn't work, at least not for me. I can't pop out a good idea on command, I've gotta wait for something to come to me. At heart I don't think this one's especially compelling and it was about two weeks before I even had this one come to me. I'm sure other people have approaches, I don't have one developed yet.

It's a collaborative writing site, and anything you write here will only be improved by collaboration. Good luck! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a good point. In it's current state, any opaque container will work, even an envelope. I think I'll rework that. Thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Boooo. Jumping out a window is SUCH a superhero-y thing to do. Especially when surrounded by billowy cape!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
See, I downvoted mostly because of the one-size-fits all thing. It's just too much - they make you want to put them on, they fit everyone perfectly, and then they make you delusional? It feels too on-rails for me.

In all fairness I probably feel that way because I structured my first SCP the same way, and after feedback came to understand why that's a shortcut to thinking. I get the compulsion to wear them, as they'd otherwise be discarded, but stretching to fit anyone? That's too much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this, but I am a sucker for superhero themes. It would be intriguing if they didn't stretch, so you would have someone just have it over one leg, or their head.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: IMO, the author needed to include the compulsion, or else the first person to wear them would die, and the underwear would be thrown out/destroyed..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked it. The idea that the item gives compulsion without means to act on it is rather entertaining, yet does have implications. What if someone with power, like an O5, suddenly wanted to use their endless resources for vigilante justice?

+1'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed. This is made even worse by the compulsion effect. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh. This is SCP 572, except with superhero underwear instead of the katana.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this, not sure yet if it's due to the writing or my own personal love of comic books and superheroes. I also admire the way you accept and respond to criticism. Makes me think that writing my first entry doesn't have to be so scary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Downvote. I just hate the idea. Like desperately, agonizingly hate it, and there's nothing else that could be done to change my mind. Sorry

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It seems to me like "Specimens of SCP-1388 are to be stored in opaque containers" really ought to work. There's no need to specify a size: containers contain, so whatever size they are, they're going to be big enough to hold however many pairs the Foundation acquires; what's important is not that they be exactly 20 cm long but that people can't see through them.

If you really need to specify a size so the cardboard-box-getting guy can get an appropriate cardboard box, you could say "Specimens of SCP-1388 are to be stored in opaque containers with minimum dimensions of 5 x 5 x 15 cm." That way, if the closest two sizes in the cardboard box storage area are 4 x 6 x 18 cm and 8 x 8 x 25 cm, cardboard-box-getting guy knows he's got to choose the second one. But I really don't think you should have to specify this: anybody reading the SCP probably (hopefully) has some idea of the dimensions of a pair of underwear and could make a reasonable guess all on their own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Regarding the first point, see an earlier comment (and the revision history). I can't decide on the right terminology for a plain, opaque, 20cm cubic container.

Regarding the second point, I don't see a tremendous difference in the passage with or without quotes; I think the context makes it clear the responses are being gathered from test subjects. The idea is that they all give very similar responses, but I'm not sure on how (or if) I want to tackle additional addenda, logs and so forth; I don't think the premise is strong enough to tack on a lot of additional data and not come off as hacky.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Specimens of SCP-1388 are to be stored in opaque 20cm small item containers.

I don't understand this phrase. Does '20cm small item containers' mean the dimension of the containers is '20cm'? Because unless it's one dimensional, this is wrong. What if '20cm small' was describing the word 'item', so that the container contained items at least 20cm in… wait, that doesn't make sense either. This shit doesn't make sense. Fix it.

have given responses related to nostalgia and/or the thrill of being a child again

This may be a personal quibble but then I see 'the thrill of being a child again', and it not being in quotes or anything, I'm going to take it like it is. And as it is, it's bad tone. Perhaps you can involve an interview log in which the person uses such words and then later note that this is pretty much universally accurate to every other interview or something? Or put it in quotes or something?

Overall, don't feel much for the SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So THIS is where all those downvoted X-men SCPs really come from…

item.title: Re: This needs funny experiment logs. Now.
item.content:encoded: Right, because what this SCP needs is more funny. It reads to me like a flat -J.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was in the first draft, and incited a general brawl (in both the story and the critique). I removed it. ;)

item.title: Re: This needs funny experiment logs. Now.
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, don't do test logs. Tale would be much better for this.

Edit: Also, I will forever think of this as the magical blender-man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oh my god yes

item.title: Re: This needs funny experiment logs. Now.
item.content:encoded: I would recommend against doing test logs in the context of an addition to the SCP, but it could make for a funny tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To go with the super-hero theme, the underwear should need to be worn over your pants.

item.title: Re: This needs funny experiment logs. Now.
item.content:encoded: I might come back to it with some logs, they're not there right now. Thanks. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't watch House but that's funny. It's not such a wildly interesting idea. Appreciate the input.

item.title: This needs funny experiment logs. Now.
item.content:encoded:
Right. Fucking. Now.

I can't be any more straightforward than that. It's the beginning of a great article. Forget frightening, go for hilarious. Not "lolfoundation" hilarious, but "this is so funny and fucked up that it has to be a real thing out there right now" hilarious. You get what I'm saying.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: House MD did this first, and it was more interesting and quite frankly more frightening. The execution is rather flat, and I'm not a fan of objects what compulse you to use them for no reason other than to activate their various effects.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like my first few will probably suck and I want to get them out of the way, and the concept wouldn't leave me alone.

Actually, I wouldn't say this sucks at all, mostly it's that the idea doesn't grab me. The fact that you're in the net positive in terms of votes indicates that you've succeeded. I'll admit to being a picky bastard when it comes to SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Understandable, I get the "crazy to death" backlash. Wanted to post it all the same; I feel like my first few will probably suck and I want to get them out of the way, and the concept wouldn't leave me alone. I got a laugh out of writing it anyway. I appreciate the explanation for the downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At heart, it's a compulsive item what crazies you to death. I have to downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the concept. The problem you're up against is that the concept is more silly than scary or mysterious. Sorry, but I can't really take the idea of scary stretchy underpants seriously.

That said, I find magic underpants a lot more interesting than a magic ring or belt or any other part of a traditional superhero costume. I'm not saying this would be better suited to a -J, just that you've got a really high hill to overcome before I'd find this item at the same emotional intensity as other favorite SCPs. Boa Noah has an interesting idea in death-by-vigilantism. I think that'd be worth exploring because there's more of a range of death-styles to play around with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The latter. And the concept didn't interest me, so it would still draw more of a neutral vote from me even if you did that. I seem to be in the minority on this one, so you're probably safe leaving it as-is. That's just how I feel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I appreciate the feedback. Any thoughts on how to better present it or are you thinking a more extreme overhaul?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The concept didn't really interest me, and I don't like the way you just rattled on with the "It does this then this then this" style of writing. Didn't flow well for me, and I always find that to be a deal-breaker.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can fly! I can fl—oops, fuck! SPLAT!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dear god why.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it! Nice and simple. Now if you don't mind, I have some flying to do!
*leaps off a building*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's also these

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I should've thought about death by vigilantism but alas. A good punchline left to waste.

EDIT: Though, the rate at which the effects manifest make it difficult for the wearer to try and stop, say, a mugging on the street. Maybe. I don't live in NYC or London though. The raised endorphins and "superpower" make them more likely to do something rash or demonstrate their powers than even have an opportunity to try and use their powers for good.

I snipped the "as though from the same package" bit, that was stream-of-conciousness and it made it through several rounds of editing. It's a little too hacky to stand scrutiny.

I appreciate the input, thanks for reading.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Currently two specimens are in Foundation custody, apparently identical as though from the same package.

I don't like this, and I think the article would be better off if it was removed.

*Specifically the "apparently identical as though from the same package" part.

However, the victim never recovered from sedation and was taken off life support October 5, 200█.

Wait, what kind of sedative are we using that it will put someone in a permanent coma?

Those aside, I'm not much of a fan of super stretchy underwear that gives people the delusion of superpowers (which leads to them killing themselves in really stupid ways rather than leading them to attempt vigilantism which then leads to stupid deaths.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the insight, the tone is surprisingly difficult to nail. I think that's a solid suggestion. Will make the change.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Since it was determined "autodefenestration" is only death by jumping from a window and not just off of a building, it seemed better to let the 5-dollar word die a graceful death and actually explain what I wanted to explain. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting concept. I would suggest, however, that you state your procedures in the affirmative, rather than by exception:

Specimens of SCP-1388 may be stored in individual standard 20cm small item containers, with the caveat that only opaque containers may be used.

This reads better as:

Specimens of SCP-1388 are to be stored exclusively in opaque 20 cm small item containers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted, although I'm disappointed mentions of autodefenestration are no longer present.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this may be the very first underwear SCP I've ever seen.
